perturbation.original_text: Sparkling's right, if any, to use the Software and Documentation at locations other than the Authorized Locations is subject to the payment of additional fees in accordance with the Fee Schedule.
perturbation.changed_text: Sparkling's right to use the Software and Documentation at locations other than the Authorized Locations is subject to separate negotiation with Garman.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the explicit link to the 'Fee Schedule' for using the software at unauthorized locations. This creates a contradiction because Section 11 states: 'Sparkling shall pay the fees as set out in the Fee Schedule in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and the Fee Schedule.' If the 'Fee Schedule' no longer governs usage outside Authorized Locations as per the change in Section 2, then Section 11 becomes unclear how fees for unauthorized locations will be determined, creating a potential in-text contradiction. Furthermore, in Section 17(a), it states the agreement remains in effect perpetually 'as long as fees are paid by Sparkling in accordance with the Fee Schedule', which is now contradict if sparkling needs to negotiate usage fee at unauthorized locations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: FEES Sparkling shall pay the fees as set out in the Fee Schedule in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and the Fee Schedule.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the explicit tie to the 'Fee Schedule' for usage outside Authorized Locations and states it is subject to separate negotiation. The contradicted text states that 'Sparkling shall pay the fees as set out in the Fee Schedule'. This creates a direct conflict because the changed text introduces a separate fee determination mechanism (negotiation) for unauthorized locations, whereas the contradicted text mandates adherence to the Fee Schedule. This contradiction creates uncertainty and potential unenforceability, as the contract now provides conflicting instructions regarding payment obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Telephone Support Service includes Sparkling having direct telephone access to employees of Garman who have the necessary technical expertise and experience to understand and consider Sparkling's inquiries concerning the failure of Software to operate according to Specifications and to clarify Documentation that is either insufficient or unclear. Such direct telephone access shall be available in accordance with the Telephone Support Schedule. In the event that the Telephone Support Service does provide a resolution to Sparkling (acting reasonably) within 2 business days to permit the Software to meet the Specifications, then Garman shall provide Software Repair Services to address the Software failure. The existing Telephone support numbers are for Garman business hours 1-800-667-6901 or 1-306-242-6322 PAGE 5 and after hours 1-306-717-1999, which is a pager number being monitored directly by qualified Garman staff.
perturbation.changed_text: Telephone Support Service includes Sparkling having telephone access to Garman. In the event that the Telephone Support Service does provide a resolution to Sparkling (acting reasonably) to permit the Software to meet the Specifications, then Garman shall provide Software Repair Services to address the Software failure.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the definition of 'direct telephone access to employees of Garman who have the necessary technical expertise and experience', as well as removing the direct reference to the 'Telephone Support Schedule' and concrete phone numbers. This creates a contradiction because the original text specifies what 'Telephone Support Service' includes, and by removing that concrete definition, makes the requirements ambiguous, despite referring to it throughout section 10. It also creates a contradiction in Section 1(f), which defines 'Modifications means the modifications set out in Schedule 'D''. If Schedule 'D' is supposed to define this, by removing concrete examples, and direct telephone access to personal with technical experience, create contradiction on what is consider to be modification.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (f) Modifications means the modifications set out in Schedule "D."
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific details about 'direct telephone access to employees of Garman who have the necessary technical expertise and experience' and the direct reference to the 'Telephone Support Schedule' and concrete phone numbers, which were present in the original text. This creates a contradiction with section 1(f), which states that 'Modifications means the modifications set out in Schedule 'D''. If Schedule D is supposed to define Modifications (which include Telephone Support), the change removes the clarity of who/what the support is. The original text provided a more concrete definition, making it clear what 'telephone support' entailed, but the revised version removes these specifics, leading to an ambiguity that contradicts the expectation of a clear modification according to schedule D.

perturbation.original_text: For the acceptance period and for a period of one year from the Maintenance Commencement Date, and thereafter for as long as the Software is covered by Maintenance Services and is used by Sparkling in accordance with this Agreement, Garman warrants that the Software shall perform in conformance with the Specifications in all material respects.
perturbation.changed_text: For a period of one year from the Maintenance Commencement Date, Garman warrants that the Software shall perform.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the warranty tied to 'the acceptance period', 'as long as the Software is covered by Maintenance Services', and 'in conformance with the Specifications in all material respects'. This creates a contradiction because Section 10 outlines the 'Maintenance Services', including the 'Software Update Service' to fix bugs and provide improvements so 'Software not operate substantially in conformance with the Sparkling's Specifications in all material respects, Garman will use its best efforts to repair the Software under the support agreement', but section 12 no longer explicitly requires Garman to ensure the Software meets 'Specifications'. This creates an in-text contradiction of whether those Specifications are still part of the terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SOFTWARE REPAIR SERVICE Should the Software not operate substantially in conformance with the Sparkling's Specifications in all material respects, Garman will use its best efforts to repair the Software under the support agreement at no additional charge to Sparkling. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Garman may charge a fee at its rates set out in the Fee Schedule to provide Software Repair Services which are required due to: (1) failure due to Sparkling operator errors; (2) failure to maintain Software at such updated version or release of the Software provided by Garman to Sparkling, from time to time, at no additional charge as part of the Maintenance Services or otherwise; (3) material modification of the Software by Sparkling; or (4) the Software being transferred to a different networking environment or hardware configuration without the prior written consent of Garman; or (5) any failure of the hardware or the operating system not caused by Garman or the Software.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the explicit requirement for the software to conform to 'Specifications', while the contradicted text still refers to 'Sparkling's Specifications'. By removing the explicit warranty tied to specifications, the changed text creates an in-text contradiction with the 'SOFTWARE REPAIR SERVICE' section, which outlines Garman's obligation to repair software that does not conform to those specifications. This aligns with the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' because essential language has been removed, creating a contradiction with obligations stated elsewhere.
